title: lavandula pedunculata var. cariensis
intro: Lavandula pedunculata var. cariensis, also known as Caria lavender, is a species of flowering plant in the lavender genus, native to Turkey and the eastern Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Caria lavender lie in its essential oils, which contain camphor, borneol, and linalool, making it useful for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of digestive disorders, such as bloating and gas. Caria lavender can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oil blends, which can be used to calm the mind and soothe the body.
